name: Lifecycle Contract
summary: How artifact lifecycle works: 3 states, runtime auto-demotes on edit, only Gatekeeper promotes to cold.

definitions.meaning: Active work in progress. The initial state for all artifacts.
distinguishing_features: Mutable by anyone with write access

definitions.meaning: LLM studio work complete. Content has passed Gatekeeper validation and is ready for human review or export.
distinguishing_features: Gatekeeper has validated and approved the content
Showrunner promotes here after GK passes all quality bars

definitions.meaning: Canonized and immutable. Final state after passing validation.
distinguishing_features: Only Gatekeeper can promote to this state

obligations.must: Validate artifacts while they are in draft state
Promote from draft to review after content passes all quality bars
Be the only one to promote from review to cold
obligations.when: Explicit approval flow requests cold promotion

obligations.must: Auto-demote artifacts from review to draft when edited
Move demoted artifacts from manuscript back to workspace

contracts.name: Cascade Demotion

obligations.must: Auto-demote child artifacts when parent is edited
Be aware that children may need re-validation after parent changes

examples: Editing a passage_brief demotes its derived passage

rules.statement: You cannot skip states - there is NO draft→cold transition
rules.reasoning: Content must pass through review first to ensure quality gates are applied.

rules.statement: Only Gatekeeper can promote artifacts to cold state
rules.reasoning: Gatekeeper judges substance via quality bars; cold promotion requires passing all checks.

heuristics.guidance: Creators work in draft. Gatekeeper validates and promotes to review. Cold promotion is a separate approval step.
examples: Scene Smith creates passage in draft → delegates to Gatekeeper → GK validates and promotes to review
Showrunner wants content validated → delegates to Gatekeeper (NOT calling lifecycle tools directly)
counter_examples: Showrunner calling request_lifecycle_transition directly without GK validation
Any agent other than Gatekeeper promoting to review or cold

warnings.failure_mode: Non-Gatekeeper agent calling request_lifecycle_transition to promote to review
warnings.consequence: Bypasses quality validation. Content may have issues that Gatekeeper would catch.
warnings.prevention: ONLY Gatekeeper promotes artifacts. Showrunner delegates to GK, never promotes directly.

warnings.consequence: Agents may think they need to validate content in review state. Review means validation ALREADY PASSED.
warnings.prevention: Remember: draft = working, review = GK passed, cold = published. Validation happens in draft.

warnings.failure_mode: Attempting cold promotion without explicit approval flow
warnings.consequence: Transition will be rejected by runtime.
warnings.prevention: Cold promotion requires explicit approval (human or formal playbook), not just GK validation.
